evals.prompt: Create a Salesforce org architecture diagram for a B2B company using Sales Cloud, Service Cloud, Experience Cloud, and Marketing Cloud. Include identity, integration middleware, ERP, and data warehouse. Output Draw.io and PNG into ./artifacts/diagrams/.
evals.expected_output: Produces a Draw.io-first architecture diagram with layered Salesforce and external systems, exports PNG, and respects requested output path.
expectations: Output includes a .drawio source artifact.
Output includes a PNG export artifact.
Artifacts are saved under ./artifacts/diagrams/.
Diagram includes all requested Salesforce clouds (Sales, Service, Experience, Marketing).
Diagram includes identity, middleware, ERP, and data warehouse nodes.
Connections represent integration/data flow between major system groups.

evals.prompt: Build an ERD for Salesforce objects: Account, Contact, Opportunity, OpportunityLineItem, Product2, Pricebook2, PricebookEntry, Quote, Order, and custom object Subscription__c. Show cardinalities and relationship directions.
evals.expected_output: Generates a Salesforce data model ERD using appropriate object and relationship conventions with clear cardinalities and directions.
expectations: Output is an ERD/data-model style diagram rather than a generic flowchart.
Diagram includes all requested objects including Subscription__c.
Relationships are drawn between relevant objects (for example Opportunity to OpportunityLineItem).
Relationship direction is indicated on connectors.
Cardinality notation is present (one-to-many or equivalent).
Custom object is visually distinguishable from standard objects or clearly labeled as custom.

evals.prompt: Map Salesforce integrations for Order-to-Cash: Salesforce to SAP S/4HANA, Stripe, Avalara, and Snowflake. Distinguish synchronous APIs vs async events.
evals.expected_output: Creates an integration topology diagram that separates synchronous and asynchronous flows with clear system boundaries.
expectations: Diagram includes Salesforce, SAP S/4HANA, Stripe, Avalara, and Snowflake.
Connectors distinguish synchronous vs asynchronous communication styles.
Legend or explicit labeling explains sync vs async semantics.
Integration boundaries or zones are visually clear.
Order-to-Cash data movement is represented end-to-end.

evals.prompt: Create a diagram for our Salesforce security model showing role hierarchy, profiles, permission sets, sharing sets, and public groups.
evals.expected_output: Builds a security/access model diagram covering hierarchy and access-control components in Salesforce.
expectations: Diagram includes role hierarchy structure.
Diagram includes profiles and permission sets as distinct control layers.
Diagram includes sharing sets and public groups.
Relationships between role hierarchy and access controls are represented.
Result is a security/access model view, not a generic architecture view.

evals.prompt: Draw a Salesforce automation flow overview for Lead-to-Cash with record-triggered flows, validation rules, Apex invocables, and approval processes.
evals.expected_output: Produces a workflow/automation diagram representing flow stages, automation components, and decision points.
expectations: Diagram reflects Lead-to-Cash progression with multiple stages.
Record-triggered flows are explicitly shown.
Validation rules are represented as control/decision components.
Apex invocables are included as automation elements.
Approval processes are included and connected in the lifecycle.

evals.prompt: Need a diagram of a generic microservices platform: API gateway, auth service, order service, payment service, Kafka, Postgres, Redis, and observability stack.
evals.expected_output: Skill should still produce a valid non-Salesforce architecture diagram in Draw.io format without Salesforce-only assumptions.
expectations: Diagram includes API gateway, auth, order, and payment services.
Diagram includes Kafka, Postgres, Redis, and observability components.
Output remains Draw.io-oriented even though request is not Salesforce-specific.
Architecture view uses service-to-service connectivity and data/event paths.
No unnecessary Salesforce-only assumptions are introduced.

evals.prompt: Please create a sequence diagram (Mermaid) for case escalation from Experience Cloud to Service Cloud and Slack notifications.
evals.expected_output: Because Mermaid is explicitly requested, output should be Mermaid sequence diagram content instead of default Draw.io-only output.
expectations: Output is Mermaid sequence diagram content.
Participants include Experience Cloud, Service Cloud, and Slack (or equivalent labels).
Sequence reflects case escalation progression.
No Draw.io-only enforcement blocks Mermaid output when explicitly requested.
Message directions are present between participants.

evals.prompt: Can you draw a quick diagram of Salesforce + NetSuite + HubSpot data flow?
evals.expected_output: Defaults to Draw.io when format is unspecified and produces an integration-style diagram.
expectations: When format is unspecified, output defaults to Draw.io workflow.
Diagram includes Salesforce, NetSuite, and HubSpot.
Data-flow connections are represented between the systems.
Result is an integration-style diagram rather than an unrelated diagram type.
No Mermaid output is produced unless explicitly asked.

evals.prompt: Create a Salesforce architecture diagram with no header. Include Sales Cloud, Service Cloud, MuleSoft, and SAP.
evals.expected_output: Generates diagram content while omitting the mandatory-by-default header because the user explicitly asked for no header.
expectations: Diagram includes Sales Cloud, Service Cloud, MuleSoft, and SAP.
Header block is omitted because user explicitly requested no header.
Architecture relationships between Salesforce and external systems are present.
Result still follows requested diagram scope despite header removal.
No contradictory instruction re-adds a mandatory default header.

evals.prompt: Use my neo-brutalist style preset and create a Salesforce capabilities map for Sales, Service, CPQ, Billing, and Field Service.
evals.expected_output: Attempts to resolve and apply named style preset while creating a capabilities map diagram.
expectations: Run attempts to resolve the named preset neo-brutalist.
Capabilities map includes Sales, Service, CPQ, Billing, and Field Service.
If preset exists, output states that preset is applied.
If preset does not exist, output reports unknown preset and available alternatives.
Diagram intent remains a capabilities map rather than an unrelated view.

evals.prompt: Generate a Salesforce architecture diagram and export SVG and PDF only to docs/architecture/.
evals.expected_output: Produces Draw.io source and only the requested SVG/PDF exports in the specified folder.
expectations: Output includes Draw.io source artifact.
Output includes SVG and PDF exports.
Exports are written to docs/architecture/.
No unrequested export format is produced (for example PNG/JPG).
Architecture content is Salesforce-focused and coherent.

evals.prompt: From Salesforce metadata, create a component map of objects, Apex classes, LWCs, Flows, and external integrations with arrows for dependencies.
evals.expected_output: Creates a metadata/component dependency map aligned to Salesforce-focused diagramming behavior.
expectations: Diagram includes objects, Apex classes, LWCs, Flows, and external integrations.
Dependencies are represented with directional arrows.
View is a component/dependency map rather than an ERD or sequence diagram.
Dependencies are logically plausible (for example Flow referencing object or Apex integration path).
Output aligns with Salesforce metadata/component mapping intent.
